blurb: Independent UK research institute on data & AI for people and society (part of the Nuffield Foundation). Strong named-author publication output across AI law/policy, public-sector AI, and public participation.

publications.title: 'I love it, but I hate it': young people's experiences and expectations of growing up digital
publications.summary: Report on young people's lived experience of a digital childhood, feeding Ada's public-participation and AI-governance work.

publications.title: Measuring up: evaluating claims about AI and productivity in the UK public sector
publications.summary: Policy briefing interrogating productivity claims made for AI in UK public services — an AI-evaluation lens on government adoption hype.

publications.title: Navigating the future: a landscape review of AI in career guidance for young people
publications.summary: Landscape review of AI tools in career guidance, examining public-sector deployment and equity implications.

publications.title: Scribe and prejudice? Exploring the use of AI transcription tools in social care
publications.summary: Report on AI transcription tools in social care — bias and evaluation risks in a sensitive public-sector setting.

notes: [publications backfill 2026-06-26] Harvested from the org's own publications page. Some items lack a visible date on the index — null rather than fabricated; refine on a later pass.
